pattern: Expanding every substring from each start while keeping a running zero/one tally answers the k-constraint check in constant time per step.
hints: A substring qualifies as soon as either its zero-count or its one-count is at most k - you don't need to know both counts precisely once one of them is confirmed small.
Fix the left end of the substring and grow the right end one character at a time, keeping a running count of zeros and ones seen so far.
For each left end, walk the right end across the rest of the string, updating the two counters and incrementing the total whenever zeros<=k or ones<=k holds.

edge_inputs.description: k equal to string length (everything qualifies)
long block of one bit followed by a long block of the other
isolated single flips inside a long run

stress.description: random binary string near max length, several k values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, k]
